Those who boast about sin are boasting about their shame. God’s patience and tolerance are abused by sinners, which hardens their hearts in their wicked ways. But enemies vainly boast of their evil deeds, while we have God's mercy to trust in. It will not save us from the guilt of lying if we can say there was some truth in what we said, but make it appear otherwise than it was. The more trickery and planning there is in any wickedness, the more Satan is involved in it. When good people die, they are transplanted from the land of the living on earth to heaven, the garden of the Lord, where they will take root forever; but when wicked people die, they are uprooted to perish forever. The believer sees that God will destroy those who do not make him their strength.

Those who think to support themselves in power and wealth without God deceive themselves terribly. The unrighteous man trusted in the abundance of his riches; he thought his wickedness would help him keep his wealth. Right or wrong, he would get what he could, and keep what he had, and ruin anyone that stood in his way; he thought this would strengthen him; but see what it comes to! Those who by faith and love dwell in the house of God will be like green olive trees there. And for us to be as green olive trees, we must live a life of faith and holy confidence in God and his grace. It adds much to the beauty of our profession and fruitfulness in every grace to praise God often; and we never can lack something to praise. His name alone can be our refuge and strong tower. It is very good for us to wait on that saving name; there is nothing better to calm and quiet our spirits when they are disturbed, and to keep us in the way of duty when tempted to use any crooked courses for relief, than to hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the Lord. None ever followed his guidance but it ended well.
